story: Me and Larry built a go-kart from Dad’s reel-mower engine. The DeWalls’ porch was full of girls with lemonade; we wanted to blast past them on the sidewalk. We tried a dumb hose prank, got caught, and ditched it. I tightened the front two-by-four so the steering board wouldn’t flop when we hit the broken slab by Mr. Sampica’s oak. From being yanked around earlier, a fine hairline crack already ran along the steering two-by-four’s grain. Larry went to flirt with Yolanda; I, the engineer, cranked everything tight. Starting and then hopping in was hard, so I backed up, sprinted in my Keds, jumped over the engine, and landed upright on the seat just as it fired. The clutch grabbed and I shot toward the DeWalls’ porch and that broken slab.
character: The narrator (the boy driving the kart)
implausible_knowledge: From being yanked around earlier, a fine hairline crack already ran along the steering two-by-four’s grain.
question: As the kart lunges forward toward the broken slab by the oak, what will he do?
answer_with_knowledge: Sit down immediately and slow or bail out to avoid stressing the weakened steering at the slab.
answer_without_knowledge: Keep standing to show off at the girls instead of slowing or stopping before the slab.
